name: ExtensionEncounter_VirtualService
title: R5: Connection details of a virtual service (e.g. conference call) (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `Encounter.virtualService` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`Encounter.virtualService` 0..* `VirtualServiceDetail`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `Encounter.virtualService` 0..* `VirtualServiceDetail`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `Encounter.virtualService` has a context of Encounter based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `Encounter.virtualService` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Connection details of a virtual service (e.g. conference call) (new)
element.comment:
Element `Encounter.virtualService` has a context of Encounter based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `Encounter`.
Element `Encounter.virtualService` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
There are two types of virtual meetings that often exist:* a persistent, virtual meeting room that can only be used for a single purpose at a time, * and a dynamic virtual meeting room that is generated on demand for a specific purpose.Implementers may consider using Location.virtualService for persistent meeting rooms.If each participant would have a different meeting link, an extension using the VirtualServiceContactDetail  can be applied to the Encounter.participant BackboneElement.

element.short: DataType slice for a FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` value
element.definition: Slice to indicate the presence of a R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` in FHIR R4

element.comment: Must be: VirtualServiceDetail

element.short: R5: Channel Type (new)
element.comment:
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `VirtualServiceDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` is not mapped.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
As this type defines what the virtual service is, it then defines what address and additional information might be relevant.

Some profiles could be used for specific types to define what the other values could/should be.
element.requirements:
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` is part of an existing definition because parent element `VirtualServiceDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` is not mapped.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.channelType` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Channel Type
element.definition: The type of virtual service to connect to (i.e. Teams, Zoom, Specific VMR technology, WhatsApp).
element.comment:
As this type defines what the virtual service is, it then defines what address and additional information might be relevant.

Some profiles could be used for specific types to define what the other values could/should be.

element.short: R5: The type of contact (new)
element.comment:
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.purpose` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ExtendedContactDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.purpose` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ExtendedContactDetail` is not mapped.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.purpose` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If no purpose is defined, then these contact details may be used for any purpose.

If there is no named individual, the telecom/address information is not generally monitored by a specific individual.

element.short: R5: This contact detail is handled/monitored by a specific organization (new)
element.comment:
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ExtendedContactDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ExtendedContactDetail` is not mapped.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
element.requirements:
Some specific types of contact information can be an handled by an organization (eg legal council is via a specific firm). Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ExtendedContactDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ExtendedContactDetail` is not mapped.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.organization` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: This contact detail is handled/monitored by a specific organization
element.definition: This contact detail is handled/monitored by a specific organization. If the name is provided in the contact, then it is referring to the named individual within this organization.

element.short: R5: Period that this contact was valid for usage (new)
element.comment:
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.period` is part of an existing definition because parent element `ExtendedContactDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.period` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `ExtendedContactDetail` is not mapped.
Element `ExtendedContactDetail.period` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
If the details have multiple periods, then enter in a new ExtendedContact with the new period.
element.requirements:

element.short: Period that this contact was valid for usage
element.definition: Period that this contact was valid for usage.
element.comment: If the details have multiple periods, then enter in a new ExtendedContact with the new period.

element.short: Contact address/number
element.definition: What address or number needs to be used for a user to connect to the virtual service to join. The channelType informs as to which datatype is appropriate to use (requires knowledge of the specific type).

element.short: R5: Address to see alternative connection details (new)
element.comment:
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.additionalInfo` is part of an existing definition because parent element `VirtualServiceDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.additionalInfo` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` is not mapped.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.additionalInfo` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This web address can be used to provide additional details on the call, such as alternative/regional call in numbers, or other associated services.

element.short: R5: Maximum number of participants supported by the virtual service (new)
element.comment:
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.maxParticipants` is part of an existing definition because parent element `VirtualServiceDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.maxParticipants` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` is not mapped.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.maxParticipants` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Some services have only a limitted number of participants permitted to connect at a time, this is typically used in "shared" virtual services that you might find on location resources.

Typically on-demand services might not have these restrictions.
element.requirements:

element.short: Maximum number of participants supported by the virtual service
element.definition: Maximum number of participants supported by the virtual service.
element.comment:
Some services have only a limitted number of participants permitted to connect at a time, this is typically used in "shared" virtual services that you might find on location resources.

Typically on-demand services might not have these restrictions.

element.short: R5: Session Key required by the virtual service (new)
element.comment:
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.sessionKey` is part of an existing definition because parent element `VirtualServiceDetail` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.sessionKey` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `VirtualServiceDetail` is not mapped.
Element `VirtualServiceDetail.sessionKey` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Some services require a session key to be able to access the service once connected.

This could be in cases where a shared number is used, and a session key is added to put into a private line, or to identify the participant in a call.
